2604.26615.title: TDD Governance for Multi-Agent Code Generation via Prompt Engineering
2604.26615.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) accelerate software development but often exhibit instability, non-determinism, and weak adherence to development discipline in unconstrained workflows. While test-driven development (TDD) provides a structured Red-Green-Refactor process, existing LLM-based approaches typically use tests as auxiliary inputs rather than enforceable process constraints. We present an AI-native TDD framework that operationalizes classical TDD principles as structured prompt-level and workflow-level governance mechanisms. Extracted principles are formalized in a machine-readable manifesto and distributed across planning, generation, repair, and validation stages within a layered architecture that separates model proposal from deterministic engine authority. The system enforces phase ordering, bounded repair loops, validation gates, and atomic mutation control to improve stability and reproducibility. We describe architecture and discuss encoding software engineering discipline directly into prompt orchestration, which we think offers a promising direction for reliable LLM-assisted development.
2604.26615.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）加速了軟體開發，但在不受限制的工作流程中，往往表現出不穩定性、非確定性和對開發紀律的弱遵循。雖然測試驅動開發（TDD）提供了一個結構化的紅-綠-重構過程，但現有的基於LLM的方法通常將測試作為輔助輸入，而非可強制執行的過程約束。我們提出了一個AI原生的TDD框架，將經典TDD原則操作化為結構化的提示層級和工作流程層級治理機制。提取的原則在機器可讀的宣言中形式化，並分佈在規劃、生成、修復和驗證階段，這些階段在一個分層架構中將模型提案與確定性引擎權威分開。該系統強制執行階段排序、有限的修復循環、驗證閘和原子變異控制，以提高穩定性和可重現性。我們描述了架構並討論了如何將軟體工程紀律直接編碼到提示編排中，我們認為這為可靠的LLM輔助開發提供了一個有前景的方向。
2604.26615.author: Tarlan Hasanli et.al.
2604.26615.authors: Tarlan Hasanli, Shahbaz Siddeeq, Bishwash Khanal, Pyry Kotilainen, Tommi Mikkonen, Pekka Abrahamsson
